description_long: This chapel is constructed primarily from dark stone blocks, giving it a robust and historic appearance. The structure features a rectangular nave with a prominent, slightly protruding entrance section, accessible by a wide staircase. A tall, narrow bell tower rises from one side of the building, with a small arched window near its top. The facade includes a circular window above the main arched doorway, and several arched windows are distributed along the sides. The rear of the chapel is characterized by a semi-cylindrical apse, typical of Romanesque architecture. The overall design is simple yet imposing, with minimal decorative elements and a focus on solid, geometric forms.
description: A dark stone chapel with a rectangular nave, arched windows, a tall bell tower, and a semi-cylindrical apse. The entrance is marked by a wide staircase and a circular window above the main door.
description_view_0: Side view showing the long nave, the bell tower on the left, and the semi-cylindrical apse at the rear.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the wide staircase, arched entrance door, and circular window above the main entry.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with visible arched windows and the main entrance section projecting forward.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the semi-cylindrical apse and the back walls of the chapel, with the bell tower visible on the right.
attribute_steeple_or_bell_tower: Present; a tall, narrow bell tower is attached to one side of the chapel, featuring a small arched window near the top and a simple rectangular form.
attribute_entrance_door_design: The main entrance door is arched, made of dark wood, and set within a stone frame. It lacks ornate decoration, emphasizing a simple, solid design consistent with Romanesque style.
